title: Block Comment with Ctrl-V + I
subtitle: Add a comment marker to N consecutive lines.
summary: Ctrl-V to enter block visual, j or count+j to select rows, I to insert at left edge, type the comment marker, Esc — the marker appears on every line.

blocks.text: Vim has no built-in "comment this block" command — but block-Visual + capital-{key:I} gives you a one-trick replacement that works in any language.

sequence.note: Insert at left edge of block

blocks.title: Why This Beats Plugins for Most Cases
blocks.text:
Plugins like vim-commentary make this prettier ({key:g}{key:c}{key:c} comments a line). But the block-Visual approach:

  • Has zero dependencies — works on any Vim, anywhere.
  • Works on any prefix marker — `//`, `#`, `--`, `;`, `// TODO:`, anything.
  • Composes with counts — {key:1}{key:0}{key:Ctrl-V}{key:j} for 11 lines.

The trick relies on the block-insert behavior: text typed after {key:I} from block Visual is *replayed* across all selected rows after {key:Esc}. See [`visual.block-insert-append`](#visual.block-insert-append).
